title: "I can mont my external hard drive"

forum: New to Ubuntu

### Post by math303 on 2013-01-05
hi everyone , I am new to ubuntu and use to have window XP and I have switch to ubuntu couple month ago , I use to have a external hard drive to back up everything ,
I have tryed to connected my wd external hard drive to my ubuntu pc with no success , so I have save my filles into a sd card and wipe out all the document , I tough because there was window doc that was what s stoping ubuntu to read it , but still no success , I have try to re-format the hard drive but this did look to do anything so here I am ask for you help ,I do really know what to do next , to help you i have some info below :
external hard drive WD 1TB

dmesg | tail -n 20```

```
mathieu@mathieu-Dimension-5100:~$ dmesg | tail -n 20
[85444.239573] sd 4:0:0:0: [sdb] Attached SCSI disk
[85559.883728] usb 1-7: USB disconnect, device number 6
[85570.860021] usb 1-7: new high-speed USB device number 7 using ehci_hcd
[85570.995386] scsi5 : usb-storage 1-7:1.0
[85571.993233] scsi 5:0:0:0: Direct-Access     WD       Ext HDD 1021     2002 PQ: 0 ANSI: 4
[85571.994178] sd 5:0:0:0: Attached scsi generic sg2 type 0
[85571.994717] sd 5:0:0:0: [sdb] 1953519616 512-byte logical blocks: (1.00 TB/931 GiB)
[85571.996001] sd 5:0:0:0: [sdb] Test WP failed, assume Write Enabled
[85571.997148] sd 5:0:0:0: [sdb] Asking for cache data failed
[85571.997156] sd 5:0:0:0: [sdb] Assuming drive cache: write through
[85572.002227] sd 5:0:0:0: [sdb] Test WP failed, assume Write Enabled
[85572.003303] sd 5:0:0:0: [sdb] Asking for cache data failed
[85572.003311] sd 5:0:0:0: [sdb] Assuming drive cache: write through
[85572.014191]  sdb: sdb1
[85572.016354] sd 5:0:0:0: [sdb] Test WP failed, assume Write Enabled
[85572.017222] sd 5:0:0:0: [sdb] Asking for cache data failed
[85572.017227] sd 5:0:0:0: [sdb] Assuming drive cache: write through
[85572.017232] sd 5:0:0:0: [sdb] Attached SCSI disk
[85586.863790] EXT3-fs (dm-0): error: can't find ext3 filesystem on dev dm-0.
[85586.880803] EXT4-fs (dm-0): VFS: Can't find ext4 filesystem
mathieu@mathieu-Dimension-5100:~$

### Post by math303 on 2013-01-05

### Post by Mark Phelps on 2013-01-05
Have to ask ... is this a Passport drive? And, if so, do you have a password set on the drive? If so, you can only access that drive through MS Windows.

---

### Post by math303 on 2013-01-05
> **Mark Phelps said:**
> Have to ask ... is this a Passport drive? And, if so, do you have a password set on the drive? If so, you can only access that drive through MS Windows.

Yes, there is a password so I guess I better off buying a new one which is compatible with Linux ,thank you for your help

---

### Post by Mark Phelps on 2013-01-05
Sorry to say this, but the ever-popular Passport drives are basically incompatible with Linux because WD installs a front-end on the drive which only works with Windows.

What you can TRY, is removing the password when the drive is mounted in Windows.  Folks have had some luck accessing the drive in Linux after that.

---

### Post by scratman on 2013-01-05
I have a Passport drive and have never had any issues with it,  I have never set a password, but I would imagine that simply removing the password in Windows, or setting it to blank would ensble Ubuntu to mount the drive.

---

### Post by Mark Phelps on 2013-01-05
> **scratman said:**
> I have a Passport drive and have never had any issues with it,  I have never set a password ... 

The problem with those drives is that the access manager (where you set/change/remove the password) only runs in MS Windows.  So, if you set a password, there is no way to enter that password when you try to access it in Linux.

Once you remove the password, there should be no problem accessing it.

---

### Post by math303 on 2013-01-08
First , thank you to all the people how replied , 
I have resoled the problem so I tough I posted to help any of you guys :

[LIST=1]
[*]First I had to remove my  password I use to have with my XP windows to protect my external hard drive
[*]then your need to mount your external hard drive to your pc , for this your go to your nautilus and type "disk utility" click on it
[*]that should open a windows with your external hard drive , DVD player , main hard drive ,etc.. , click on your external hard drive
[*]this should give you the information your need to mount your external hard drive for if your hard drive if formated in fat32 like my was your will need to reformat in NTFS that should able you to use your hard drive with linux and windows
[/LIST]

[LIST=1]
[*]I give you the way I set up my hard drive :
[/LIST]

[LIST]
[*]usage : filesystem
[*]Partition Type: Linux (0X83)
[*]partition flags: -
[*]type: NTFS
[*]Label:"What you want to call it , I call it Math-E-H-D
[*]partition label: -
[*]capacity: 1.0 TB
[*]available: -
[*]mount point : Mounted at /media/ Math-E-H-D
[/LIST]
Ok hope hope this can help you ,

---
